title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders: end users (cleaning performance, ergonomics, runtime, noise, maintenance), buyers/retailers (price point, claims substantiation, packaging), regulatory/compliance (EU safety, EMC, batteries, chemicals, charger), service/support (replaceable parts, warranty, diagnostics), manufacturing/supply chain (battery cells, motor, plastics, charger, cost/availability), sustainability stakeholders (WEEE, battery take-back, recyclability). Current requirements specify runtime (>=20 min), mass (<1.5 kg), environment (indoor 10–40°C), EU markets, and charging base. Key requirement risks/ambiguities: runtime depends on mode/load and test method; weight definition (with/without accessories, empty vs full bin); which EU standards/Directives apply (LVD may or may not apply depending on voltage; Battery Regulation, EMC, RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Ecodesign/energy labeling not typical but check; charger safety). Missing product-level requirements: suction/airflow performance and pickup efficacy, dustbin capacity, filtration level (e.g., HEPA), noise limit, charging time and battery type/capacity, user interface indicators, durability (drop, cycles), maintainability (filter washing/replacement), safety features (thermal, overcharge, short-circuit), materials and flammability, IP/dust protection for indoor use, accessories/nozzles, storage/stand stability, and claim verification/testing protocols.
clarification_questions: How should the 20+ minute runtime be defined and verified (e.g., standard/eco mode on hard floor with empty bin; new battery at 20°C; minimum end-of-life runtime after X charge cycles)?

What cleaning performance targets are required (e.g., minimum suction power/airflow or pickup efficiency on hard floor and carpet, dustbin capacity, filtration/HEPA requirement, and maximum noise level)?

What charging and battery expectations apply (battery chemistry, max charge time, replaceable vs sealed battery, required certifications/markings for charger and product for EU—CE under which Directives/standards)?
